reasoning: The Brooklyn Nets are currently entering a deep rebuild and are widely expected to be one of the worst teams in the NBA during the 2024-2025 season. While they possess substantial expected cap space in the summer of 2025 and an arsenal of future draft picks (giving them a non-zero tail-end chance of rapidly assembling a highly competitive team via trades and free agency), jumping from a rebuilt roster to winning the Eastern Conference Finals in a single season is incredibly rare. The current market rate is about 1.35%. Adhering to the operational floor constraint, I round this to 0.02 (2%), estimating a highly improbable path.
